instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `must-be` that asserts a value satisfies a unary predicate. The value is evaluated once; an error is signaled if the predicate returns nil; otherwise the value is returned.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 NAME))
            (UNLESS (STRINGP #:G1)
              (ERROR "~S does not satisfy ~S" #:G1 'STRINGP))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 NAME))
            (UNLESS (STRINGP #:G112)
              (ERROR "~S does not satisfy ~S" #:G112 'STRINGP))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
